items.title: Deepening ties with the homeland: Multiple dimensions of the UK Sikh diaspora's role in the farmers' protest
items.book: A People's History of the Farmers Movement 2020-21
items.summary: Documents the multiple dimensions of the UK Sikh diaspora's role in the farmers' protest in India in 2020-21

items.title: Farming Laws: BJP's Strategic Vision of Strengthening Agro-business Capitalism and Hindu Nationalism
items.book: The Indian Farmers' Protest of 2020-2021: Agrarian Crisis, Dissent and Identity
items.summary: Examines the BJP-led government's three farming ordinances of 2020 and argues that they were aimed at strengthening corporate control of agriculture, centralising power, and furthering the Hinduisation agenda

items.title: The ecological consequences of the rising economic power of the BRICS economies in global capitalism: An eco-socialist perspective
items.book: Global Challenges of Climate Change, Vol. 2
items.summary: Tracks the spatial shift in global capitalism and its ecological consequences, particularly the rise of BRICS economies. Argues that the combination of rising per capita income and large populations in BRICS creates unprecedented ecological pressures, and that a transition to eco-socialism is a historic necessity

items.title: Contradictory implications of Third World poverty for global sustainability
items.book: Proceedings of the Second International Green Economics Conference
items.summary: Examines the contradictory relationship between Third World poverty and global sustainability from green economics and Marxist theoretical perspectives

items.title: Punjab and Federalism
items.book: Federalism, Nationalism and Development: India and the Punjab Economy
items.summary: Explores the interplay between federalism, nationalism, and economic development in the context of Punjab, India

items.title: Globalisation, Punjab and Identity
items.book: Punjabi Identity in a Global Context
items.editors: Pritam Singh and Shinder Singh Thandi (eds)
items.summary: Explores the dynamics of Punjabi identity in the context of globalisation, addressing issues of diaspora, multiculturalism, and transnational identity

items.title: Capital, State and Nation in Indian Federalism: With special emphasis on Punjab
items.book: Class and Its Intersectionalities
items.summary: Examines the interplay of capital, state and nation in Indian federalism with special emphasis on Punjab
items.notes: In Press at time of CV.

items.title: How Centre-State Relations Shape Punjab's Development Pattern?
items.book: Economic Transformation of a Developing Economy: The Experience of Punjab, a State in India
items.summary: Analyses how centre-state relations have shaped Punjab's development pattern

items.title: Institutional Communalism in India
items.book: The Changing Forms of Communalism in Modern India
items.summary: Examines institutional forms of communalism in India

items.title: Experiencing torture and human rights violations: reflections on self-experience
items.book: Human Rights in Postcolonial India
items.summary: Personal reflections on experiencing torture and human rights violations in a postcolonial context

items.title: Inspired and Distorted Role of Media
items.book: Embedded Journalism: Punjab
items.summary: Examines the inspired and distorted roles of media in reporting on Punjab

items.title: Sikh Culture and Punjābiyat
items.book: The Oxford Handbook of Sikh Studies
items.editors: Pashaura Singh and Louis E. Fenech (eds)
items.summary: Examines Sikh culture and the concept of Punjābiyat (Punjabi identity)

items.title: The rise and fall of the welfare state in the UK
items.book: Cracovian Studies in Social Work
items.summary: Analyses the historical trajectory of the welfare state in the UK
items.notes: 75% contribution by Singh and 25% by Bhusal.

items.title: The Impact of International Remittances on Reduction of Poverty in Bangladesh and Ecuador
items.book: Encyclopaedia of Global Human Migration (4 Volumes)
items.editors: I Ness, A Julca et al. (eds)
items.summary: Examines the impact of international remittances on poverty reduction in Bangladesh and Ecuador

items.title: The Rise of Sikh Belief and Community
items.book: World History Encyclopedia
items.summary: Overview of the historical rise of Sikh belief and community

items.title: Interrogating Marxian, Neo-classical and Green Perspectives on the Contradictory Implications of Third World/Asian Development and Poverty
items.book: Sustainability of Asian Economic Development
items.editors: Sucha Singh Gill, Lakhwinder Singh and Reena Marwah (eds)
items.summary: Critical examination of Marxian, neo-classical and green perspectives on development and poverty in the Third World and Asia

items.title: Global Food Crisis as a Part of the Crisis of Global Capitalism
items.book: Globalization and Change
items.summary: Analyses the global food crisis as a manifestation of the broader crisis of global capitalism

items.title: Political Economy of Centralisation in India: Some Critical Notes from a Decentralist Perspective
items.book: Nationalizing Crisis: The Political Economy of Public Policy in Contemporary India
items.summary: Critical notes on the political economy of centralisation in India from a decentralist perspective

items.title: Sectarianism and Human Rights Discourse: The Politics of Human Rights in the Post-Colonial Punjab
items.book: Changing Concepts of Rights and Justice in South Asia
items.summary: Examines the politics of human rights discourse in post-colonial Punjab through the lens of sectarianism

items.title: Punjab's Economic Development and the Current Crisis
items.book: Punjab: Past, Present and Future
items.summary: Analyses Punjab's economic development trajectory and the challenges of the current crisis

items.title: What is a Sikh Problem?
items.book: The National Question in Punjab (in Punjabi)
items.summary: Examines the nature of the Sikh problem in the context of the national question in Punjab

items.title: Forming a Women's Group in Chandigarh: Experiences of a Male Activist
items.book: Women in Indian Society: A Reader
items.summary: Personal reflection on the experience of a male activist in forming a women's group in Chandigarh

items.title: Two Facets of Revivalism
items.book: Punjab Today
items.summary: Examines the two facets of religious and cultural revivalism in Punjab

items.title: Role of Media
items.book: Punjab in Indian Politics
items.summary: Analysis of the role of media in shaping political discourse in Punjab

items.title: Assam Problem: Towards a Framework of Analysis
items.book: North-East Region: Problems and Prospects of Development
items.publisher: Centre for Research in Rural and Industrial Development, Chandigarh
items.summary: Develops a framework for analysing the Assam problem in the context of development in India's North-East

items.title: Imperialism, Indian Capitalism and Punjab Economy
items.book: Punjab Economy (in Punjabi)
items.summary: Analyses the relationship between imperialism, Indian capitalism and Punjab's economy

items.title: Towards a Perspective for Women's Movement
items.book: Women and Society Problems and Prospects
items.summary: Develops a perspective for the women's movement in India
